items.front: When an IFR flight plan is filed directly with a center and kept on voice recording, what must be entered on the strip?
items.back: The sector or position number in box 26 of the initial flight progress strip.

items.front: What must be recorded when flight plan information is forwarded manually?
items.back: The time of filing and the time of delivery on the form.

items.front: When should other VFR flight plan data be forwarded?
items.back: Only if the pilot requests it.

items.front: Which DVFR departure times must be forwarded to the military operations office?
items.back: All DVFR departures from joint-use airports.

items.front: What should ATC do if a pilot wants to change from an IFR to a VFR flight plan?
items.back: Request the pilot to contact the appropriate FSS.

items.front: When must IFR flight progress data normally be forwarded to the receiving facility?
items.back: At least 15 minutes before the aircraft is estimated to enter the receiving facility's area, unless an LOA or facility directive changes the timing.

items.front: When manually entering a flight plan that already has a computer-assigned beacon code, what must you do?
items.back: Insert that beacon code in the appropriate field as part of the input message.

items.front: When an aircraft is part of an approved ALTRV, what information should be forwarded?
items.back: Only the items needed to identify the flight, update ALTRV data, or revise previously given information.

items.front: If your facility cannot automatically acknowledge a transferred computer message, what must you obtain?
items.back: A Service F acknowledgment that the receiving center got the message and verified the required data.

items.front: When must NADIN be used for a proposed flight plan during inter-center computer operations?
items.back: When the route of flight exceeds 20 elements external to the originating ARTCC's area.

items.front: If route or altitude is amended within 30 minutes of proposed departure, how must the receiving facility be coordinated with?
items.back: By both verbal and automated means; if automation is unavailable, verbal coordination alone is sufficient.

items.front: Which airborne military IFR changes must be forwarded to flight service?
items.back: IFR flight plans, VFR-to-IFR changes, destination changes, and fuel-exhaustion time changes.

items.front: What lead time is normally used for proposed departures and en route estimates between domestic U.S. ARTCCs and Canadian ACCs?
items.back: Thirty minutes, or another bilaterally agreed lead time.

items.front: What format do Canadian ACCs use to send flight data to FAA ARTCCs?
When must "NRP" be removed from a flight plan's remarks?
items.back: When the route of flight is altered because of a pilot request.
